node_types.description: An individual involved in therapy, such as a client or therapist.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role of the person (e.g., client, therapist, family member)

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Cultural or religious background, including Islamic alignment if relevant

node_types.description: A specific emotional state experienced by a person.

properties.name: mood_type
properties.description: Type of mood (e.g., depression, anxiety, anger, guilt, shame, happiness)

supported_by_chapters: 15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame
Worksheet 15.1. Measuring and Tracking My Moods

node_types.description: A cognitive event, such as an automatic thought, hot thought, or core belief.

properties.name: thought_type
properties.description: Type of thought (automatic, hot, core belief, underlying assumption, balanced, alternative)

properties.name: believability_rating
properties.description: How much the person believes the thought (0–100%)

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance
Worksheet 12.6. Core Belief Record: Recording Evidence That Supports a New Core Belief
Worksheet 12.7. Rating Confidence in My New Core Belief

node_types.description: A specific context or event relevant to a person's experience.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the situation (who, what, when, where)

properties.name: environment_type
properties.description: Type of environment (e.g., home, work, social)

properties.name: life_event
properties.description: Whether the situation is a significant life event

node_types.description: An action or set of actions performed by a person, including coping, avoidance, or safety behaviors.

properties.name: behavior_type
properties.description: Type of behavior (e.g., coping, avoidance, safety, assertive, reparative)

properties.name: effect_on_mood
properties.description: Observed effect of the behavior on mood

supported_by_chapters: 15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame
Worksheet 12.8. Rating Behaviors on a Scale
Worksheet 12.9. Behavioral Experiments to Strengthen New Core Beliefs

node_types.description: A physiological response associated with a mood or situation.

properties.name: reaction_type
properties.description: Type of physical reaction (e.g., rapid heartbeat, sweating, muscle tension)

node_types.description: A structured instrument or worksheet used for assessment or tracking.

properties.name: tool_name
properties.description: Name of the assessment tool (e.g., Depression Inventory, Anxiety Inventory, Thought Record)

supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 13.1. Mind Over Mood Depression Inventory
Worksheet 14.1. Mind Over Mood Anxiety Inventory
Worksheet 15.1. Measuring and Tracking My Moods
Worksheet 16.2. My Plan to Reduce Relapse Risk

node_types.description: A structured plan for therapeutic intervention, including goals, action steps, and relapse prevention.

properties.name: relapse_prevention_plan
properties.description: Plan for maintaining gains and preventing relapse

supported_by_chapters: 5. Setting Personal Goals and Noticing Improvement
10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance
16. Maintaining Your Gains and Experiencing More Happiness
Worksheet 16.2. My Plan to Reduce Relapse Risk

node_types.description: A specific therapeutic technique or exercise applied in treatment.

properties.name: intervention_type
properties.description: Type of intervention (e.g., behavioral experiment, exposure, mindfulness, relaxation, gratitude, forgiveness)

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance
15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame
Worksheet 11.2. Experiments to Test an Underlying Assumption
Worksheet 12.9. Behavioral Experiments to Strengthen New Core Beliefs

node_types.description: A psychological or behavioral issue experienced by a person.

properties.name: problem_type
properties.description: Type of problem (e.g., depression, anxiety, anger, guilt, shame, substance use, relationship issue)

supported_by_chapters: 15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame

node_types.description: A personal value or principle that guides behavior and treatment planning.

properties.name: value_text
properties.description: Description of the value (e.g., family, faith, honesty)

properties.name: cultural_alignment
properties.description: Alignment with cultural or religious principles, including Islamic values if relevant

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person experiences a specific mood.
supported_by_chapters: 15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame
Worksheet 15.1. Measuring and Tracking My Moods

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is experiencing a specific psychological or behavioral problem.
supported_by_chapters: 15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person performs a specific behavior.
supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 12.8. Rating Behaviors on a Scale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has a specific thought, such as an automatic thought or core belief.
Indicates that a person experiences a specific physical reaction.
Indicates that a mood is triggered by a specific situation.
Indicates that a mood is associated with a specific thought.

properties.name: association_strength
properties.description: Strength of the association (e.g., hot thought rating)

edge_types.description: Indicates that a thought is targeted or challenged by a specific intervention (e.g., cognitive restructuring, behavioral experiment).
supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 11.2. Experiments to Test an Underlying Assumption

edge_types.description: Indicates that a thought arises in response to a specific situation.
Indicates that a behavior has an effect on a mood.

properties.name: effect_type
properties.description: Type of effect (e.g., improvement, worsening, neutral)

edge_types.description: Indicates that an assessment tool is used to measure a mood.

properties.name: score
properties.description: Score or rating produced by the assessment

supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 13.1. Mind Over Mood Depression Inventory
Worksheet 14.1. Mind Over Mood Anxiety Inventory
Worksheet 15.1. Measuring and Tracking My Moods

edge_types.description: Indicates that an assessment tool is used to evaluate a specific problem.

properties.name: score
properties.description: Score or rating produced by the assessment

supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 13.1. Mind Over Mood Depression Inventory
Worksheet 14.1. Mind Over Mood Anxiety Inventory

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person uses or completes an assessment tool.
supported_by_chapters: Worksheet 13.1. Mind Over Mood Depression Inventory

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has a specific treatment plan.
supported_by_chapters: 5. Setting Personal Goals and Noticing Improvement
10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance
16. Maintaining Your Gains and Experiencing More Happiness

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan includes a specific intervention.

properties.name: sequence_order
properties.description: Order of the intervention in the plan

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person holds or endorses a specific value.

properties.name: importance_rating
properties.description: How important the value is to the person

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance

edge_types.description: Indicates that an intervention is designed to address a specific problem.

properties.name: effectiveness_rating
properties.description: Effectiveness of the intervention for the problem

supported_by_chapters: 10. New Thoughts, Action Plans, and Acceptance
15. Understanding Your Anger, Guilt, and Shame
